zone_6b_notes: Missouri native perennial — among the best plants for pollinators in Zone 6b. Blooms July–August in shades of red, pink, and lavender. Spreads by rhizome and will colonize a bed over 3–4 years; divide every 3 years to control spread and maintain vigor. Powdery mildew is the main issue in Missouri humidity — Jacob Cline (red, mildew-resistant) and Marshall's Delight (pink, mildew-resistant) are the best cultivars for humid summers. Plant in morning sun with afternoon shade or in well-ventilated spots to reduce mildew. Hummingbirds visit red-flowered types intensively in July and August.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant divisions or transplants in spring or fall; direct sow in fall

water: consistent — 1–1.5 inches/week; drought increases mildew susceptibility
